name: Calorie Calculator
titleTag: Calorie Calculator - Daily Calorie Needs | Smart Calculator Pro
metaDescription: Calculate your daily calorie needs based on age, gender, height, weight, and activity level. Plan your diet effectively.
intro: Calculate your daily calorie needs to maintain, lose, or gain weight based on your personal details and activity level.
shortDescription: Calculate daily calorie needs for weight goals.
longDescription: This calculator estimates your Total Daily Energy Expenditure (TDEE) using the Mifflin-St Jeor equation, considered the most accurate formula for most people.
problemSolved: Helps users understand their daily calorie needs for weight management.

inputs.label: Gender
inputs.explanation: Your biological sex affects BMR calculation.

outputs.label: Daily Calorie Needs (TDEE)
outputs.explanation: Calories needed to maintain current weight.

outputs.label: For Weight Loss
outputs.explanation: Calories for losing ~1 lb per week.

outputs.label: For Weight Gain
outputs.explanation: Calories for gaining ~1 lb per week.

formula.label: Mifflin-St Jeor Equation
formula.plainText: Total Daily Energy Expenditure equals Basal Metabolic Rate multiplied by activity level.

formulaExplanation: BMR for men: (10 × weight in kg) + (6.25 × height in cm) - (5 × age) + 5
BMR for women: (10 × weight in kg) + (6.25 × height in cm) - (5 × age) - 161
TDEE is calculated by multiplying BMR by your activity level
methodologyNotes: This calculator uses the Mifflin-St Jeor equation, considered most accurate for most people
Activity multipliers: Sedentary (1.2), Light (1.375), Moderate (1.55), Active (1.725), Very Active (1.9)
Individual variations in metabolism are not accounted for

example.scenario: A 30-year-old woman, 68 inches tall, weighing 150 pounds, with moderate activity.
example.resultSummary: Her BMR is approximately 1,488 calories. With moderate activity, her TDEE is approximately 2,306 calories per day.

resultInterpretation.defaultText: Your TDEE represents the calories you burn daily. To lose weight, eat fewer calories than your TDEE. To gain weight, eat more. For maintenance, eat approximately your TDEE.
warningMessages: Don't drop calories too low—eating below 1,200 calories for women or 1,500 for men may be harmful.
Weight loss of 1-2 pounds per week is considered healthy and sustainable.
Individual results may vary; consult a healthcare provider or registered dietitian for personalized advice.

faq.question: What is BMR?
faq.answer: BMR (Basal Metabolic Rate) is the number of calories your body needs at complete rest to maintain basic functions like breathing and circulation.

faq.question: What is TDEE?
faq.answer: TDEE (Total Daily Energy Expenditure) is your BMR plus calories burned through physical activity. It represents your total daily calorie needs.

faq.question: How many calories should I eat to lose weight?
faq.answer: Eating 500 calories below your TDEE typically results in losing about 1 pound per week. A sustainable approach aims for 1-2 pounds of weight loss per week.

disclaimer: This calculator provides estimates only and is not a substitute for professional medical or nutritional advice. Individual calorie needs may vary based on metabolism, genetics, and other factors.
